prompt:
Help frame the research described in the benchmark case as a concrete business-research problem. Preserve the supplied model and identify claims or information that remain unsupported.

Condition instructions:
Respond as you normally would to this user request using only the serialized case material. Do not assume facts that are not supplied.

Benchmark case:
```json
{
  "adversarial": true,
  "decision_time_facts": [
    "revenues",
    "capacity consumption",
    "estimated transition matrix"
  ],
  "domain": "OM",
  "evidence_needs": [
    {
      "claim": "Transitions describe substitution",
      "source_needed": false,
      "status": "model_assumption"
    }
  ],
  "fidelity_requirements": [
    "Transition paths are states, not choices",
    "Capacity applies to selected products"
  ],
  "hard_failure_traps": [
    "state_as_decision",
    "model_changed_for_story",
    "future_or_hidden_information"
  ],
  "id": "om-02",
  "model_specification": {
    "constraints": [
      "category capacity limits"
    ],
    "decision_variables": [
      "binary assortment membership"
    ],
    "mathematical_objective": "maximize expected assortment revenue",
    "mechanism": "When a preferred item is absent, the modeled transition process reallocates choice probability across alternatives until purchase or exit.",
    "model_type": "capacity-constrained Markov chain choice model",
    "state_variables": [
      "customer transition path",
      "final purchased alternative"
    ],
    "uncertainty": "customer starting state and transition path"
  },
  "prohibited_inferences": [
    "The retailer can steer each customer's realized transition"
  ],
  "prompt": "A retailer selects an assortment before demand realizes. Customers transition among unavailable alternatives according to a Markov-chain choice model, but every product consumes a category-specific capacity unit. Explain the business decision and mechanism without converting transition probabilities into managerial controls.",
  "tags": [
    "markov-chain-choice",
    "assortment",
    "capacity",
    "adversarial"
  ],
  "title": "Capacity-constrained Markov-chain assortment"
}
```
